name: LoanStar Title Loans
description_short: LoanStar Title Loans offers auto title loans up to $10,000 across Texas. Founded in 1990, they use your vehicle title as collateral with minimal documentation.
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has grown into a multi-state title lending operation with a significant presence in Texas. The company positions itself as one of America's longest-standing vehicle equity lenders, emphasizing its track record of helping working Americans access cash against the value of their cars. In Texas, LoanStar operates under licensing and oversight from the Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner (OCCC), and is required to publish fee schedules for its loan products.

LoanStar's core product is the auto title loan, also called a car title loan. Borrowers use their vehicle as collateral to secure cash loans of up to $10,000. The application requires three things: the vehicle itself, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear vehicle title — meaning the car must be fully paid off with no existing lien. Loans are structured as either single-installment or multiple-installment products, with fee disclosures available for representative amounts of $600, $1,200, and $2,000. Payment is accepted in-store via cash, check, or debit card, as well as through Western Union or the company's online payment portal.

LoanStar operates multiple locations within San Antonio, with at least five branches within an 11-mile radius of the 6001 West Avenue location — giving it meaningful geographic coverage in the metro area. Store hours run Monday through Friday 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM, which is narrower than some competitors. The website offers a Spanish-language option, reflecting the demographics of its San Antonio customer base. OCCC licensing and published fee schedules indicate legal compliance with Texas state consumer credit regulations.

As a title lender, LoanStar occupies a high-cost segment of consumer lending. The OCCC-mandated fee disclosures signal that these are expensive products — title loans in Texas frequently carry triple-digit APRs depending on loan term and amount. The primary risk is vehicle repossession: if a borrower cannot repay, they can lose their car. LoanStar is a viable option for vehicle owners with a clear title who need fast cash and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives, but borrowers should read the fee schedules carefully before signing.
address: 2450 N Belt Line Rd Suite 130, Irving, TX 75062

company_info.headquarters: 2450 N Belt Line Rd Suite 130, Irving, TX 75062

pros: Founded in 1990 — over 35 years in the title lending industry
Loans up to $10,000 — higher ceiling than typical payday or emergency cash products
Simple three-item requirement: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title
Multiple San Antonio locations (at least 5 branches within 11 miles)
Accepts four payment methods: cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
OCCC-licensed with published fee schedules for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts
Spanish-language website option serving bilingual customers
Online payment portal available for repayment convenience
cons: Vehicle must have a clear title — borrowers with an existing auto loan or lien cannot qualify
Title loans typically carry triple-digit APRs; OCCC fee disclosures confirm costs but do not minimize them
Risk of vehicle repossession if the loan is not repaid — a serious consequence for borrowers who rely on their car
Limited hours (closes 6 PM weekdays, 2 PM Saturday, closed Sunday) compared to 24/7 online lenders
Geographic coverage appears limited to Texas based on available location data
best_for: Vehicle owners with a fully paid-off car title who need $500–$10,000 quickly
Borrowers with damaged or no credit who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit cards
San Antonio-area residents who prefer in-person transactions with a physical branch
Spanish-speaking consumers who need title loan access in their primary language
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best for Texas vehicle owners who hold a clear car title, need cash fast, and cannot qualify for lower-cost credit products. The main caveat is that title loans are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit and carry the real risk of losing your vehicle if you default — borrowers should exhaust personal loans, credit unions, and payday alternatives before applying.
services: Auto title loans (car title loans) up to $10,000
OCCC-published fee schedule disclosures for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts
Multi-location branch network across San Antonio, TX
